The Fed's seven governors, including the central bank chief and vice chairs, are nominated by the president and confirmed by the Senate. Each of them votes at every Federal Open Market Committee meeting, held eight times a year.

Miran, Waller and Bowman are Trump nominees. Barr, Jefferson and Cook - whom Trump is attempting to fire - were nominated by former President Joe Biden. Powell was initially nominated to the Fed's Board of Governors by former President Barack Obama, elevated to the top Fed job by Trump in his first term, and renominated to that position by Biden. Trump plans to nominate former Fed Governor Kevin Warsh to succeed Powell when his chair term ends in mid-May.

All 12 regional Fed presidents discuss and debate monetary policy at the meetings, but only five cast votes, including the New York Fed president and four others who vote for one year at a time on a rotating schedule. Fed regional bank presidents are picked by the directors of their own regional banks, subject to approval by the Fed's board.

data.title: Cubix Announces Unified AI-Powered Platform to Drive Next Phase of Performance in Self Storage
data.description:
DANVILLE,Calif., April 2, 2026 /PRNewswire/ -- Cubix Asset Management (Cubix) today announced the rollout of a unified platform integrating Prorize, Storagely, Swivl AI, and Storage Defenders into a single, seamless operating system that redefines how self-storage assets are marketed, managed, and monetized. The milestone reflects Cubix's continued evolution as a full-stack powerhouse, delivering outsized, measurable performance gains and sustained value creation across storage portfolios.

At the core of this platform is the Cubix Demand Engine, an AI-powered, fully integrated acquisition and revenue optimization system built for performance-driven self-storage portfolios. The Demand Engine orchestrates search visibility, targeted advertising, AI-driven chat and voice engagement, e-rental conversion, and machine learning-based pricing into a single, measurable system. By continuously aligning demand, conversion, and pricing, it drives qualified leads, increases occupancy, lowers cost per move-in, and delivers real-time performance insights across every property.

The rollout comes at a pivotal moment for the industry. As the market transitions into a more stabilized and competitive environment, operators and investors are increasingly prioritizing operational efficiency, pricing discipline, and data-driven execution. Technology-enabled platforms and centralized intelligence are setting new performance benchmarks, reshaping how assets are managed and scaled.

By integrating high-performance digital experiences, AI-powered engagement, and intelligent security into one system, Cubix enables operators to manage and optimize the entire customer lifecycle from initial search and lead generation to conversion, tenant experience, and ongoing facility performance within a single connected platform.

For investors, this approach introduces a scalable and repeatable framework for enhancing asset-level performance. By aligning demand generation, conversion, and pricing through the Cubix Demand Engine, the platform is designed to improve lead quality, increase conversion rates, and optimize revenue strategies, supporting stronger net operating income and long-term asset performance.

"When technology and disciplined operations work together, performance follows. Our 2024–2025 results show that smart automation and data-driven pricing can drive NOI gains even when the broader market is flat," said Sean Venezia, Partner at Cubix Asset Management.

The platform leverages AI-driven automation to improve speed-to-lead, enhance customer engagement through chat and voice interactions, and dynamically adjust pricing based on real-time market conditions. Integrated data and analytics provide continuous visibility into performance, enabling faster and more informed decision-making across portfolios.

The result is a more intelligent, responsive, and resilient operating model that continuously adapts to market conditions while maintaining flexibility at the asset level.

The platform is currently rolling out across select portfolios, with phased expansion planned throughout 2026 as Cubix continues to scale its unified operating system across the self-storage market.

For more information about Cubix Asset Management's services, visit https://www.cubixassetmanagement.com

About Cubix Asset Management

Cubix Asset Management, also known as Cubix Storage, is a technology-driven self-storage platform and asset management company with a growing presence across California and the Western United States. With over 80 years of combined industry experience, the company has been involved in the design, development, and operation of more than 300 self-storage facilities and currently manages a portfolio of over 50 properties, representing approximately 3.3 million square feet and more than 25,500 storage units. By combining AI-powered marketing, intelligent pricing, and operational expertise, Cubix delivers measurable improvements in occupancy, revenue, and net operating income, helping operators and investors maximize performance across storage portfolios.

data.title: Tesla Expands EV and Energy Reach in Q1
data.description:
Tesla, Inc. ( TSLA ) shares are down during Thursday’s premarket session after the company reported production and delivery figures for the first quarter of 2026.

The news comes as the broader market experienced a decline, with the Consumer Discretionary sector also facing pressure, adding to the stock’s downward movement as investors digest the latest figures.

Delivers 358,000 Vehicles

In the first quarter, Tesla produced over 408,000 vehicles and delivered over 358,000 vehicles, while also deploying 8.8 GWh of energy storage products.

In particular, the company reported Model 3/Y production and deliveries of 394,611and 341,893, respectively.

This performance is part of the company’s ongoing efforts to scale production and meet growing demand for electric vehicles and energy solutions.

China-Made EV Sales

Tesla reported an 8.7% year-over-year increase in China-made EV sales to 85,670 units in March, marking a fifth straight month of growth, according to the China Passenger Car Association.

First-quarter sales rose 23.5%, accelerating from the previous quarter, while global deliveries are expected to rebound nearly 10% from last year's slump tied to backlash against CEO Elon Musk's political views.

Tesla is also expanding beyond EVs, focusing on solar energy, humanoid robots, and robotaxis, and is in talks to buy $2.9 billion in solar equipment from Chinese firms.

Peers Sales

Tesla rival Rivian Automotive Inc. ( RIVN ) (NASDAQ:RIVN) fell consecutively for 4 months in the U.S. as the electric automaker gears up to launch the awaited R2 Crossover SUV.

Another major peer, Nio Inc. ( NIO ) (NYSE:NIO) delivered 35,486 vehicles in March (+136.0% year-over-year) and 83,465 vehicles (+98.3% year-over-year) in the first quarter.

TSLA at $365.23 Below 20-Day, 100-Day

The broader market saw losses on Wednesday, with the Consumer Discretionary sector ranked 10 out of 11 sectors, declining 1.86%. Tesla’s decline occurred as the sector struggled, indicating that company-specific factors may be influencing its stock performance.

At $365.23, Tesla is trading 5.9% below its 20-day simple moving average (SMA) and 14.2% below its 100-day SMA, suggesting a bearish short-term trend. The stock’s position below these key moving averages indicates potential weakness, as it reflects a struggle to maintain upward momentum.

The relative strength index (RSI) currently sits at 46.79, indicating neutral momentum, which suggests that the stock is neither overbought nor oversold at this time. This positioning may lead to increased volatility as traders assess the stock’s next move.

  • Key Resistance: $416.50 — A level where selling pressure has historically emerged.
  • Key Support: $325.50 — A potential floor where buying interest may increase.

Tesla has a 12-month performance return of 34.84%, reflecting a solid upward trend over the past year. However, the stock’s current price is closer to its 52-week low of $214.25 than its high of $498.83, indicating a significant pullback from its peak.

Consumer Discretionary Drops 1.86%; TSLA Lags

Tesla is currently underperforming relative to its sector, with the Consumer Discretionary sector declining 1.86% on Wednesday. This sector has struggled over the past 30 days, down nearly 4%, indicating broader challenges in consumer spending and sentiment.

Despite the overall sector weakness, Tesla’s production and delivery numbers highlight its ongoing efforts to capture market share in the electric vehicle space. The company’s ability to navigate these challenges will be crucial as it seeks to maintain its competitive edge.

TSLA Earnings April 21; EPS Seen 34¢

Looking further out, the next major catalyst for the stock arrives with the April 21, 2026 (estimated) earnings report. Analysts expect earnings per share of 34 cents, up from 27 cents in the same quarter last year, and revenue of $22.85 billion, up from $19.34 billion in the prior-year period.

  • EPS Estimate: 34 cents (Up from 27 cents)
  • Revenue Estimate: $22.85 billion (Up from $19.34 billion)
  • Valuation: P/E of 353.0x (Indicates premium valuation)

Analyst Consensus & Recent Actions: The stock carries a Buy Rating with an average price target of $418.28. Recent analyst moves include:

  • Canaccord Genuity: Buy (Lowers Target to $420.00) (March 31)
  • GLJ Research: Sell (Maintains Target to $24.86) (March 30)
  • Wedbush: Outperform (Maintains Target to $600.00) (March 27)

Top ETFs Hold TSLA Up to 9.94%

  • ARK Next Generation Internet ETF ( ARKW ) : 9.94% Weight
  • American Century Focused Dynamic Growth ETF ( FDG ) : 8.84% Weight
  • iShares Global Consumer Discretionary ETF ( RXI ) : 9.19% Weight

Significance: Because TSLA carries such a heavy weight in these funds, any significant inflows or outflows for these ETFs will likely force automatic buying or selling of the stock.

data.title: Palm Beach Tan and WELLNESSâ¢ Accelerates National Expansion with Acquisition of 14 Bodyheat Tanning Locations in Las Vegas
data.description:
DALLAS, April 2, 2026 /PRNewswire/ -- Palm Beach Tan and Wellness, the nation's largest indoor tanning provider, today announced the acquisition of fourteen Bodyheat Tanning salon locations in Las Vegas. The acquisition supports the Company's continued national expansion and strengthens its presence in a key metropolitan market. Palm Beach Tan and Wellness currently operates 648 franchised and company-owned locations across 35 states.

Eight of the acquired locations will be rebranded as Palm Beach Tan and Wellness salons and will be remodeled and enhanced with new services and equipment. The remaining six locations will merge with existing nearby locations.

All sessions, packages, and memberships previously purchased at Bodyheat will be honored at all Las Vegas Palm Beach Tan and Wellness locations. Memberships will be integrated into the Palm Beach Tan and Wellness Premier Rewards® program, which provides members with benefits including service upgrades, sunless tanning options, and product discounts. Guests eager to experience Palm Beach Tan and Wellness services need not wait; the Company currently operates five existing Las Vegas locations already offering the full suite of wellness services, available throughout the conversion period over the coming months.

In addition to tanning and spray tanning, Palm Beach Tan and Wellness now offers wellness services, including red light therapy and infrared saunas, designed to support skin rejuvenation, boost collagen production, reduce inflammation, and promote overall wellness. The company also offers its proprietary Premier Collection® product line of exclusive tanning and sunless tanning products as well as its luxury skincare brand L'obsidienneX®, featuring Exosome Skincare Technology, a patented formulation that supports skin repair and reduces visible signs of aging.

"Expanding into Las Vegas is more than just growth for Palm Beach Tan and Wellness—it's a chance to connect with a vibrant community that loves to look and feel its best," said Daniel Caskey, Chief Executive Officer of Palm Beach Tan, Inc. "We can't wait to welcome new clients and offer them top-tier tanning and wellness experiences across the city."

As part of this expansion, Palm Beach Tan and Wellness is also rolling out its refreshed brand identity across the entire Las Vegas market, with updated signage reflecting the Palm Beach Tan and Wellness name and logo. This rebrand reflects the Company's evolution and commitment to delivering a comprehensive wellness experience companywide.

New Palm Beach Tan and Wellness locations include:

  • 6050 N Decatur
  • 7120 N Durango
  • 10220 W Charleston Blvd
  • 9809 W Flamingo Rd
  • 10600 Southern Highlands Pkwy
  • 9075 S Eastern Ave
  • 595 College Dr
  • 1195 Wellness Pl

ABOUT PALM BEACH TAN and WELLNESS™:
Founded in 1990, Palm Beach Tan and Wellness is the leader in the indoor beauty, wellness, and tanning industry, with 648 corporate and franchise-owned locations nationwide. The company has revolutionized the industry through innovative technology and premium products. Palm Beach Tan offers rewards-based memberships and a full suite of tanning and wellness services, including sunbed and sunless tanning, red light therapy, and infrared saunas. For more information, visit www.palmbeachtan.com
